events.name: ThemeCreated
events.trigger: New Theme row inserted. Row starts is_active=false; a separate ThemeActivated fires once someone flips is_active=true.

events.name: ThemeActivated
events.trigger: Theme.is_active transitions false → true (via ThemeObserver::updating). Fires AFTER the prior active theme (same mode) has been flipped to is_active=false in the same DB txn.

payload.tokens_snapshot_hash: SHA-256 of the frozen tokens jsonb — for cache-key derivation
payload.prior_theme_id: thm_ULID | null — the theme that was deactivated in the same transaction

events.name: ThemeDeactivated
events.trigger: Theme.is_active transitions true → false. Typically fires alongside a sibling ThemeActivated for the replacement theme in the same DB txn.

events.name: ThemePresetCreated
events.trigger: New ThemePreset row inserted. tenant_id NULL = platform preset (fires only during platform-admin operations); non-null = tenant custom preset.

payload.name: string
payload.is_platform: bool — derived from tenant_id IS NULL

events.name: ThemePresetPublished
events.trigger: ThemePreset.is_active transitions false → true. For tenant custom presets: makes the preset visible to admin selection UI. For platform presets: cross-tenant announcement.

events.name: ThemeTokenOverridden
events.trigger: New ThemeTokenOverride row inserted. One event per (theme_id, token_key) — batched at the request level.

events.name: ThemeTokenOverrideRemoved
events.trigger: ThemeTokenOverride hard-deleted or soft-deleted. Falls through to the underlying preset value in next resolution.

events.name: ThemeAccessibilityFlagChanged
events.trigger: Theme.accessibility_flags jsonb changes. One event per boolean flip within the payload — high_contrast, reduced_motion, larger_text, focus_visible_always.

events.name: ThemeCacheInvalidated
events.trigger: Fires post-RegenerateRenderedCssJob completion. Downstream consumers (CDN purge, SSR cache) key off this to bust their own caches.

payload.compiled_css_url: string — signed URL to the newly-rendered CSS

events.name: UserThemePreferenceUpdated
events.trigger: user::user_theme_preferences row updated — mode preference (light/dark/auto) OR accessibility overrides. Event fires HERE (not in user module) because theme is the resolver — user module owns the ROW, theme owns the SEMANTICS.
